compiled_state.title: [Preplanning Phase 2.5]: Blue team filter triggered -> orchestrator applies filter inline instead of dispatching dso:blue-team-filter sub-agent
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Context

During preplanning Phase 2.5 (Adversarial Review), after the red team sub-agent returns findings, the skill instructs the orchestrator to dispatch a blue team filter via \`subagent_type: \"dso:blue-team-filter\"\` with \`model: sonnet\`. The blue team filter exists specifically to prevent false positives from polluting the story map — its independence from the orchestrator is the whole point.

## Actual Behavior

The orchestrator applied the blue team filter inline (evaluated each finding itself) instead of dispatching the \`dso:blue-team-filter\` sub-agent. This occurred because the named sub-agent type \`dso:blue-team-filter\` was not resolvable via the Agent tool in this context, but the orchestrator did not fall back correctly — it did not read \`plugins/dso/agents/blue-team-filter.md\` inline and dispatch a general-purpose agent with that prompt. Instead it simply performed the filtering logic itself, defeating the independence guarantee.

## Expected Behavior

After red team returns findings, the orchestrator dispatches \`dso:blue-team-filter\` (either as a named sub-agent or via the documented fallback: read \`plugins/dso/agents/blue-team-filter.md\` inline and dispatch as a general-purpose agent with the full prompt). The blue team sub-agent independently evaluates each finding against the 4 filtering criteria and returns accepted/rejected arrays with rationale.

## Impact

The blue team filter's value is its independence — an orchestrator filtering its own red team findings is subject to confirmation bias (accepting findings that confirm its own analysis, rejecting findings that contradict it). In this session, all 12 red team findings were accepted with no rejections, which may reflect genuine quality but also may reflect the orchestrator not rigorously applying the rejection criteria.

## Fix Direction

In Phase 2.5 Step 2, after verifying the named sub-agent is unavailable, the orchestrator must:
1. Read \`plugins/dso/agents/blue-team-filter.md\` inline
2. Dispatch via the Agent tool as a general-purpose agent with the full blue-team-filter prompt and red team findings injected
3. NOT perform inline filtering as a fallback when the sub-agent dispatch fails

The same issue likely exists for the red team dispatch (Phase 2.5 Step 1) — verify both paths follow the documented agent fallback pattern.
